chunks.text: Bounds on Backtrack Algorithms for Listing Cycles, Paths, and Spanning Trees (M433) by Ronald C. Read and R. Endre Tarjan. Analysis of Calendaring Strategies for Criminal Jury Trials in Court Systems (M423) by Michael Stonebraker. Testing Graph Connectivity (M422) by R. Endre Tarjan. Cities as Public Goods (M417) by Roland Artle. An Overview of the VERS2 Project: High Level Languages in Automatic Programming (M416) by Jay Earley. Latent Components in the EKG (M414) by Martin Graham. On Worst-Case Costs for Dynamic Data Element Security Decisions (M413) by Franklin G. Woodward and Lance J. Hoffman.
chunks.question: What are the topics of the first seven technical reports from 1973?
chunks.answer: Algorithms, court systems, graph theory, urban economics, programming, EKG analysis, security.

chunks.text: BUDS: The Berkeley Urban Data System (M412) by Philip Pasquale Macri. The Concept of a Linguistic Variable and its Application to Approximate Reasoning (M411) by L.A. Zadeh. On the Solutions of a Stochastic Control System II (M406) by Tyrone Dunan and Pravin Varaiya.
chunks.question: What fields do reports M412 to M406 cover?
chunks.answer: Urban data, fuzzy logic, antennas, stochastic processes, plasma physics.
